skill.name: maxhub-threads
skill.description: A Claude, Codex, and Claude Code skill for searching Threads posts and collecting public user data via MaxHub API proxy.
skill.summary: Search Threads posts and collect public user data through MaxHub API proxy with intelligent API chaining.
skill.author: MaxHub Team

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 80 potential issues, but manual review confirms all are false positives. The backtick/Ruby execution flags are markdown inline code markers. System reconnaissance flags are markdown table syntax. The high-entropy flags are from Chinese characters in YAML frontmatter. The skill legitimately accesses MAXHUB_API_KEY for authentication and makes HTTPS requests to a documented API proxy service. Security declarations are clear and appropriate.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Authentication
low_findings.description: Skill accesses MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable for API authentication. This is legitimate and documented. The skill explicitly declares it only reads MAXHUB_API_KEY and MAXHUB_BASE_URL, with clear security documentation stating no credential storage or forwarding to third parties.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Static pattern detected env_access but this is standard API client behavior - the skill legitimately needs an API key to authenticate with MaxHub API proxy. Security documentation is clear and appropriate.

low_findings.title: Network Access to API Proxy Service
low_findings.description: Skill makes HTTPS requests to MaxHub API proxy service. URLs point to documented endpoints (aconfig.cn, gitee.com repositories). This is expected behavior for an API integration skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network access is to legitimate API endpoints documented in the skill. Security declarations state only communication with user-configured MAXHUB_BASE_URL via HTTPS.

content.user_title: Search Threads Posts and Collect Public User Data
content.value_statement: Need to search Threads posts or analyze user profiles? This skill provides intelligent access to Threads public data through MaxHub API, with automatic API chaining for complex queries.
actual_capabilities: Search Threads posts by keyword (top and recent)
Get user profile information by username or user ID
Fetch user posts, reposts, and replies lists
Get post details and comments by post ID or URL
Chain multiple API calls for comprehensive analysis
limitations: Only accesses public data via MaxHub API proxy
API calls are billed per use with rate limits (default 60/minute)
Cannot perform engagement actions (likes, comments, reposts)

use_cases.title: Research Threads Content for Market Analysis
use_cases.description: Search for trending topics, analyze popular creators, and gather engagement metrics for market research or content strategy.
use_cases.target_user: Market researchers, content strategists, social media managers

use_cases.title: Track Influencer Performance on Threads
use_cases.description: Look up specific creators, analyze their post history, track engagement metrics, and compare performance across users.
use_cases.target_user: Influencer marketing teams, brand managers, PR professionals

use_cases.title: Aggregate Threads Data for Reports
use_cases.description: Collect posts about specific topics, compile user statistics, and generate structured data for reports or dashboards.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Topic Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for recent Threads posts about [TOPIC]. Show me the top results with post content and engagement metrics.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick search for content on a specific topic

prompt_templates.title: User Profile Lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Find information about the Threads user [USERNAME]. Get their bio, follower count, and recent posts.

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Creator Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare [CREATOR1] and [CREATOR2] on Threads. Include follower counts, average engagement, and their top performing posts.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comparing multiple creators side by side

prompt_templates.title: Trend Analysis with Chain Calls
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the top 5 trending posts about [TOPIC] this week. Then get the full content and engagement data for each, and identify common patterns or themes.

output_examples.input: Search for popular Threads posts about AI art
output_examples.output: Found 10 trending posts about AI art. Top post by @creator123 has 2.5K likes, 340 reposts. Other posts discuss AI art tools, tutorials, and examples. Most recent from 2 hours ago.
Profile: @techinfluencer | Followers: 45.2K | Following: 892 | Bio: Tech reviewer & gadget enthusiast | Verified: Yes | Recent post: 'New smartphone review coming tomorrow...'

best_practices: Start with specific search queries before drilling down to details
Check account balance on MaxHub before running batch queries
Use the chain call patterns documented in references for complex analyses
anti_patterns: Do not request private user data or protected content
Avoid high-frequency automated polling that could trigger rate limits
Do not attempt to use the skill for platform manipulation or spam

faq.question: What API key do I need for this skill?
faq.answer: You need a MAXHUB_API_KEY from aconfig.cn. Register on their platform and create an API key in your dashboard.

faq.question: Does this skill post or interact with Threads content?
faq.answer: No. This skill only reads public data. It cannot post, like, comment, or perform any engagement actions.

faq.question: How many API calls can I make?
faq.answer: MaxHub API has default rate limits of 60 calls per minute. The skill warns you when pagination exceeds 5 pages.

faq.question: What data can I access?
faq.answer: Public posts, user profiles, comments, and search results. Private content and protected accounts are not accessible.

faq.question: Is there a cost to use this skill?
faq.answer: MaxHub API calls are billed per use. Check your account balance regularly, especially before batch operations.

faq.question: Can I analyze historical data or deleted posts?
faq.answer: No. Only currently public and accessible content can be retrieved. Deleted posts and historical snapshots are not available.
